description:
Advances: specled.decisions, specled.modal_class

Deliverable:
Extend the decision/requirement/scenario schemas with the structured fields that AppendOnly needs; add the pure ModalClass classifier; introduce the CrossField validator behind DecisionParser.parse_file/2; ratify the 12 new finding codes in a budget ADR. Land everything before AppendOnly wiring starts.

Concrete additions:
- decision schema: `change_type` (optional enum: deprecates | weakens | narrows-scope | adds-exception | supersedes | clarifies | refines), `reverses_what` (optional string), `replaces` (optional list of ids).
- requirement schema: `polarity` (:positive | :negative, optional), `refines` (optional id), `supersedes` (optional id).
- scenario schema: `execute` (optional boolean), `reason` (optional string).
- new module `SpecLedEx.DecisionParser.CrossField` with `validate(decision, current_index, opts) :: [error]` implementing 7 rules (Appendix A of the refined plan).
- new module `SpecLedEx.ModalClass` with pure `classify/1` and total `downgrade?/2`. No version function, no cache.
- new ADR `.spec/decisions/specled.decision.append_only_finding_budget.md` justifying 12 codes (10 append_only/* + 2 overlap/*) against `specled.decision.finding_code_budget`.
- update `.spec/specs/decision_governance.spec.md` with requirements for the enum, the 4-value weakening set, CrossField rules, and change_type optionality.

Verification:
- Tests to pass: mix test test/specled_ex/modal_class_test.exs test/specled_ex/decision_parser/cross_field_test.exs test/specled_ex/schema_test.exs
- Behaviors to demonstrate:
  - specled.modal_class.classify_total (modal_class.spec.md scenarios)
  - specled.modal_class.downgrade_total, downgrade_monotonic, cross_polarity_conservative
  - CrossField rules R1..R7 emit expected errors on crafted inputs; idempotence property (same input -> same errors) from T8 #4.
  - decision schema accepts the new fields; requirement and scenario schemas accept the new optional fields without breaking existing fixtures.
- Spec verification stubs to flip from execute: false to execute: true: every stub in modal_class.spec.md plus the CrossField additions authored under decision_governance.spec.md.

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/specled_ex/append_only.ex (belongs to W2a)
- lib/specled_ex/overlap.ex (belongs to W2b)
- lib/specled_ex/branch_check.ex (belongs to W2b)
- lib/specled_ex/config.ex (belongs to W3)
- lib/mix/tasks/spec.check.ex (belongs to W3)
- .spec/decisions/*.md other than the new budget ADR (W1.5 backfills the 19 legacy ADRs)

Allowed touches (this stage may modify):
- lib/specled_ex/schema/decision.ex
- lib/specled_ex/schema/requirement.ex
- lib/specled_ex/schema/scenario.ex
- lib/specled_ex/decision_parser.ex
- lib/specled_ex/decision_parser/cross_field.ex (new)
- lib/specled_ex/modal_class.ex (new)
- .spec/specs/decision_governance.spec.md
- .spec/specs/modal_class.spec.md
- .spec/decisions/specled.decision.append_only_finding_budget.md (new)
- test/specled_ex/modal_class_test.exs (new)
- test/specled_ex/decision_parser/cross_field_test.exs (new)
- test/specled_ex/schema_test.exs or equivalent existing schema tests

Out of Scope (intent):
- No AppendOnly behavior lands yet; detectors stay out until W2a.
- No change to existing 19 ADRs (W1.5 handles backfill after this stage).
- Do not introduce caching of modal_class on requirement structs or in state.json (explicitly rejected by specled.decision.modal_class_diff_time).
- Do not add a `modal_class_version` field (explicitly cut per refined plan B4).
- Do not widen the finding-code budget beyond the 12 ratified in the new ADR.

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly
- [ ] Tests listed above pass
- [ ] mix spec.validate green (CrossField rules do not flag the new ADR against itself)
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs (mechanical)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: d11fdf91-3678-40b7-be2d-9b876c7a2774
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/d11fdf91-3678-40b7-be2d-9b876c7a2774.jsonl

Commit: f78b653
Files touched:
- lib/specled_ex/schema/decision.ex, requirement.ex, scenario.ex
- lib/specled_ex/decision_parser.ex
- lib/specled_ex/decision_parser/cross_field.ex (new)
- lib/specled_ex/modal_class.ex (new)
- .spec/specs/decision_governance.spec.md (extended)
- .spec/specs/modal_class.spec.md (status draft -> active, execute true)
- .spec/decisions/specled.decision.append_only_finding_budget.md (new)
- test/specled_ex/schema_test.exs (extended)
- test/specled_ex/decision_parser/cross_field_test.exs (new)
- test/specled_ex/modal_class_test.exs (new)
- .spec/state.json (regenerated by spec.validate)

Tests added:
- SpecLedEx.ModalClassTest (classify, downgrade, totality, transitivity)
- SpecLedEx.DecisionParser.CrossFieldTest (R1..R7 pass/fail, idempotence)
- SpecLedEx.SchemaTest: describe blocks for decision change_type,
  requirement optional fields, scenario execute/reason

Verification stubs flipped (false -> true):
- modal_class.spec.md: both command and source_file stubs
- decision_governance.spec.md: new stubs authored with execute: true

Summary: W1 ships structured ADR schema (change_type enum + associated
fields), the CrossField validator wired through DecisionParser, the
pure ModalClass module, and the finding-budget ADR. Every must
requirement in specled.modal_class has a passing test; the new
specled.decisions requirements are covered by CrossField and schema
tests. No AppendOnly or Overlap behavior lands in this commit.

Note on spec.check: seven warnings remain and all belong to
append_only.spec.md / overlap.spec.md surfaces and verification
targets that fm4.3 (W2a) and fm4.4 (W2b) will create. These warnings
were introduced by the epic-setup commit (e6fb1bc) and are outside
fm4.1's Allowed touches. mix spec.validate status=pass; mix test
445 tests passing.

Resolved spec contradiction: cross_polarity_conservative was
internally inconsistent with downgrade_monotonic when read literally.
Rewrote the cross_polarity_conservative requirement to make the
negative-to-positive transition explicitly non-downgrade (delegated
to the separate negative_removed detector). This keeps downgrade?
acyclic and therefore monotonic. Not authored as a separate ADR
because it narrows the requirement to match the acyclic-partial-order
intent already stated by downgrade_monotonic and the scenario
fixtures.

VERIFY: PASS
Session: a4f9619414b5f3b8b (subagent of d11fdf91-3678-40b7-be2d-9b876c7a2774)
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/d11fdf91-3678-40b7-be2d-9b876c7a2774/subagents/agent-a4f9619414b5f3b8b.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 13
- Files outside Allowed globs: 1 (.spec/state.json) — mechanically regenerated by `mix spec.validate`, not hand-authored; ticket merge gate "mix spec.validate green" requires this regeneration. Treating as allowed by implication.
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0. append_only.ex and overlap.ex do not exist. branch_check.ex, config.ex, mix/tasks/spec.check.ex and the 19 pre-existing ADRs were NOT modified. mix.exs was NOT modified.

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 2 command stubs (both `execute: true`) + 3 source_file stubs = 5. All corresponding tests/files run and pass.
- Requirements with passing tests: 17/17
  - specled.modal_class: 6/6 — classify_total (totality loop test), classify_deterministic, classify_case_and_punctuation_insensitive (case + punctuation tests), downgrade_total (49-pair test), downgrade_monotonic (transitivity loop test), cross_polarity_conservative (pos→neg + neg→pos tests).
  - specled.decisions: 11/11 — frontmatter_contract + reference_validation covered by pre-existing decision_parser_test / verifier_test; change_type_enum (accept/reject), weakening_set (weakening_types/0 == 4), change_type_optional (R7 warning), cross_field_supersedes_replaces (R1×3), cross_field_reverses_what (R2×3), cross_field_affects_non_empty (R3×2), cross_field_affects_resolve (R4+R6), cross_field_adr_append_only (R5×8), cross_field_idempotent (idempotence×2). Each requirement maps to a distinct passing assertion that exercises the rule (not just loads the module).
- Intent-level scope concerns: none.
  - No lib/specled_ex/append_only.ex or overlap.ex created.
  - No new mix deps.
  - No `modal_class` field cached in state.json or on the Requirement struct (grep shows only subject-id references like `specled.modal_class.*`, never `"modal_class": value` cache entries).
  - No `modal_class_version` field anywhere (only appears in ADR prose explicitly cutting it).
  - Finding-code budget: exactly 10 append_only/* + 2 overlap/* = 12, matching the ratified ADR.
- Project verification: mix spec.validate=pass (errors=0, warnings=7). mix spec.check=fail (errors=0, warnings=7). All 7 warnings are surface_target_missing / verification_target_missing for lib/specled_ex/append_only.ex, overlap.ex, test/specled_ex/append_only_test.exs, overlap_test.exs, test/support/append_only_fixtures.ex — the companion source files that fm4.3 (W2a) and fm4.4 (W2b) will create. Verified via `git show e6fb1bc:.spec/specs/{append_only,overlap}.spec.md`: both spec files exist at the prior commit (e6fb1bc, epic setup) with status `draft` and surface entries already pointing at the not-yet-existing files. fm4.1 does not author or modify either spec file. Warnings pre-date this commit and are outside fm4.1 Allowed touches.
- Spec change judgement (cross_polarity_conservative rewrite): ACCEPT as a narrowing.
  (a) Monotonic: making neg→pos non-downgrade while pos→neg IS a downgrade keeps the relation acyclic; the old text was internally inconsistent because "any cross-polarity transition that loses a negative assertion" would make neg→pos a downgrade, contradicting the explicit `:must_not → :must is false` pair and creating a cycle that breaks downgrade_monotonic.
  (b) Scenarios `must_to_must_not_is_downgrade` and `must_not_to_must_is_not_downgrade` are still respected exactly as authored — the rewrite preserves those explicit pairs verbatim.
  (c) AppendOnly handoff is clean: the polarity-loss case is now caught by `specled.append_only.negative_removed` (code ratified in the budget ADR) through the newly-added `polarity` field on the requirement schema. The Requirement struct gains `polarity :: :positive | :negative` in this same commit, so the downstream detector has the field it needs.
  The rewrite is narrowing, not weakening: the old statement was contradictory on its face; the new statement resolves the contradiction in favor of the acyclic-partial-order intent stated by downgrade_monotonic and already baked into the scenario fixtures.

Verdict: PASS

Gaps: none.

Notes:
- state.json regeneration is outside the Allowed-touches list but is a mechanical consequence of the schema additions and required by the `mix spec.validate green` merge gate. Flag for the ticket author only if future tickets want to pin this explicitly in Allowed touches.
- Decision.statuses includes `superseded` (not just `accepted` + `deprecated`); the `@statuses ~w(accepted deprecated superseded)` line in schema/decision.ex covers the full three-state transition model required by R5 and the adr_append_only ADR. Commit message says "statuses extended with 'deprecated'" but the actual extension includes both deprecated and superseded, which is correct per the ADR.
- The CrossField module emits two variant codes for R1 (`supersedes_missing_replaces` and `supersedes_unresolved_replaces`) and two for R5 (`adr_field_drift` and `adr_status_regression`). These are parse-time error code strings, not detector-level finding codes in the budget ADR; the budget ADR's 12 codes are AppendOnly/Overlap runtime emissions. No conflict.

AUDIT: APPROVE
Session: d11fdf91-3678-40b7-be2d-9b876c7a2774 (audit subagent)
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/d11fdf91-3678-40b7-be2d-9b876c7a2774.jsonl

Reasoning:

1) state.json regeneration is a legitimate mechanical consequence, not a scope violation. The merge gate requires mix spec.validate green, and spec.validate itself writes .spec/state.json (confirmed: `find . -maxdepth 1 name '*.jsonl'` showed my run regenerated it). Prior commits show state.json is touched routinely alongside spec changes (39 hits in recent W-style commits). Allowed by implication is the right call.

2) cross_polarity_conservative rewrite is a narrowing, not a weakening. I constructed the contradiction from the original text myself: old clause A says `:must_not -> :must is downgrade false`; old clause B says `any cross-polarity transition that LOSES A NEGATIVE ASSERTION shall be classified as a downgrade`. Going `:must_not -> :must` loses the negative assertion, so B says it IS a downgrade, directly contradicting A. Separately, if both `:must -> :must_not` and `:must_not -> :must` were downgrades, transitivity via downgrade_monotonic would make `:must -> :must` a downgrade, violating the identity refute test. The narrowing picks the acyclic reading already baked into the must_not_to_must_is_not_downgrade scenario fixture and delegates polarity-loss to append_only.negative_removed through the new polarity field. Scenario fixtures unchanged.

3) spec.check failure is acceptable. Confirmed via `git show e6fb1bc:.spec/specs/append_only.spec.md` that both append_only.spec.md and overlap.spec.md exist at the prior commit with surfaces pointing at lib/specled_ex/append_only.ex etc. fm4.1 did not author or modify either spec file, and both are explicitly Out of Scope. The 7 warnings are pre-existing and scheduled to be cleared by fm4.3 (W2a) and fm4.4 (W2b). Ticket gate is mix spec.validate, which is green (errors=0).

4) 17/17 must requirements have tests that would fail if the requirement were violated. Spot-verified: classify_total (modal_class_test.exs:63-80 asserts result in modals() for every sample), classify_case_and_punctuation_insensitive (lines 27-41), downgrade_total (lines 141-147 iterates the 49 pairs), downgrade_monotonic (lines 149-158 transitivity loop), cross_polarity_conservative (lines 107-110 and 119-124, both directions), R5 adr_append_only (cross_field_test.exs:217-276, including the critical is-disabled-when-nil test at 263-275), R5 status transitions (235-261 covers accepted->deprecated, accepted->superseded allowed, deprecated->accepted rejected), R7 warning severity (cross_field_test.exs:299-313), idempotence (331-365 includes both byte-identical and multiset-equality properties). Pre-existing requirements frontmatter_contract and reference_validation covered by decision_parser_test.exs:6-47 and verifier_test.exs:320-375.

5) Intent-scope checks all clean. No append_only.ex or overlap.ex (ls errored). No mix.exs/mix.lock changes (empty git show). No `"modal_class":` atom cache in state.json (grep count 0; only subject-id references like specled.modal_class.*). No modal_class_version field in lib/ (only appears in ADR prose explicitly cutting it per B4). No append_only/* or overlap/* runtime codes leaked into lib/specled_ex/ (grep returned empty).

6) ModalClass correctness sanity-checked against code, docstring, and scenarios: downgrade?(:none, :must)=false (prior==:none branch), downgrade?(:must, :none)=true (current==:none branch), downgrade?(:must_not, :must_not)=false (identity), downgrade?(:may, :must_not)=true (pos->neg), downgrade?(:must_not, :may)=false (default branch after no pos->neg match), downgrade?(:should, :shall_not)=true (pos->neg). Within negative family, must_not rank=6 > shall_not rank=5, so must_not->shall_not is downgrade (weakening a prohibition), shall_not->must_not is not — consistent with within-family weakening rule.

7) CrossField R5 has both the nil case and the fires case. Line 263-275 asserts NO adr_field_drift or adr_status_regression when prior_decisions is not supplied, and lines 217-261 assert the errors fire when it is. Also the two variant codes (supersedes_missing_replaces/supersedes_unresolved_replaces, adr_field_drift/adr_status_regression) are parse-time codes, distinct from the 12 runtime finding codes ratified in the budget ADR. No leak.

8) Budget ADR ratifies exactly 10 append_only/* + 2 overlap/* = 12 codes, matching ticket. Not exceeded.

Verdict stands.

title: W1: Structured ADR schema + CrossField + ModalClass + finding-budget ADR
